body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: acurite-free ecosystem replacement
body.intention: I intend that the acurite-free replacement is verified to actually work end-to-end with real hardware.
body.description:
**What changed:** acurite-free repo: RTL-SDR capture daemon + web dashboard replacing AcuRite app/cloud.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- rtl_433 version differences across distros may change JSON field names (temp_F vs temperature_F etc) — parse defensively
- Some cloud storage public URLs block cross-origin fetch (CORS) — users hit this and don't know why
- Neighbor sensors may flood CSV if whitelist not configured — CSV grows fast
- WU upload may silently fail if station_id/key wrong — no feedback in kiosk mode
- RTL-SDR dongle may require udev rules for non-root access — undocumented gotcha

**Signals to watch:** Does --discover actually find sensors? Does weather.csv grow with real readings? Does weather.html load and display from a public cloud URL?

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-28 or when first end-to-end test with real hardware runs.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none
**Scope boundary:** Observe and annotate predicted effects as occurred/clear/partial
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
